principles.text: Prefer existing standards, libraries, and conventions; keep changes surgical and add no mechanism or configurability that does not serve the goal.
principles.title: Substance and minimum mechanism

principles.text: Inject shared collaborators and keep each branching domain concept in one authoritative decision function.
principles.title: Dependency injection and one switch

principles.text: Domain stages, services, and stores do not import web, command-line, or UI response types; translation happens at the edge.
principles.title: Domain logic stays transport-free

principles.text: New stage, service, and input-output boundaries use Pydantic models and validate required fields before downstream typed access.
principles.title: Typed boundaries and trusted interiors

principles.text: Malformed required input, impossible state, wiring failure, and corrupt model output raise typed exceptions with context.
principles.title: Fail loud on contract breach

principles.text: Required collaborators are required in signatures, and persistent writes return the stored record or raise.
principles.title: Required dependencies are required

principles.text: Use asynchronous network, file, and delay primitives in async code and explicitly own and clean up resources.
principles.title: Async I/O and owned resources

principles.text: Use top-level package imports and structlog with keyword context for new structured logging; do not hide circular dependencies.
principles.title: Imports and logging reveal structure

principles.text: Develop on a topic branch, run required checks, open a pull request, and merge through protected main without force-admin or deploy-checkout edits.
principles.title: Protected change flow

principles.text: New dependencies, schemas, hooks, policy, root directories, spend, or authorization boundaries require explicit approval.
principles.title: Ask before changing governance

principles.text: A repeated claim, memory, prior message, tool success, or HTTP 200 is not verification; cite current primary evidence.
principles.title: Evidence before belief

principles.text: Fresh host, session, and pane discovery is required before a nudge, takeover, close, or spawn; stale live authority fails closed.
principles.title: Live reads authorize live actions

principles.text: Facts need evidence, normative claims need binding principles, and conclusions need an explicit reasoning link.
principles.title: Separate facts, rules, and inference

principles.text: Test the recorded done_when; deployable work normally requires checks, merge, deploy, and live verification, while other task classes use their explicit artifact contract.
principles.title: Completion is goal-specific

principles.text: Old run state and chat memory are not present-cycle proof; a completion claim names the current artifact and evidence.
principles.title: Current receipts only

principles.text: Freeze the exact current goal version before reasoning; tactical activity and model summaries cannot rewrite intent, goal, done_when, scope, source, or session reference.
principles.title: Preserve the original strategic contract

principles.text: Infer the smallest reversible step necessary for the recorded outcome and consistent with scope; ask only when the missing fact materially changes the decision.
principles.title: Fill gaps, do not invent direction

principles.text: Inspect, recommend, design, build, deploy, and send authorize different actions; never silently act beyond the goal's verb.
principles.title: Act at the verb's altitude

principles.text: Chitra core observes, queues, dispatches, verifies delivery, and gates completion without calling a model; adapter reasoning must not take over lane work.
principles.title: Chitra orchestrates and lanes execute

principles.text: Research resolvable facts first and escalate only genuine strategic or authorization choices with one crisp, evidence-anchored question.
principles.title: Escalate a decision, not uncertainty theater

principles.text: Tmux injection is the only write channel to an attached lane; check pane mode, paste safely, and verify the directive in the transcript.
principles.title: One writer per live session

principles.text: Goal, question, evidence, principles, candidate, author, reviewers, and verdict have stable bindings; any mutation invalidates release.
principles.title: Reasoned output is immutable after review

principles.text: Each answer names the goal version, evidence references, principle IDs, inference, confidence, and unresolved unknowns.
principles.title: Every answer exposes its basis

principles.text: Lead with the outcome, define jargon once, keep lane directives concrete, and explain options and consequences in operator decisions.
principles.title: Plain directive self-contained communication

principles.text: Missing goals, stale principles, conflicts, or absent evidence block autonomous normative answers while safe factual observations and precise missing-evidence statements remain allowed.
principles.title: Fail closed but answer boundedly

principles.text: Read the nearest package hub and compile both registry paths and filesystem-discovered hubs because the registry alone is incomplete.
principles.title: HFR is discovery plus scoped authority

principles.text: Accepted and implemented ADRs bind only their scope; draft, proposed, historical, superseded, archived, and unknown material does not silently become policy.
principles.title: ADR status matters

principles.text: Code and doctrine go in Git, project knowledge in Wiki, hot documents in DocsHome, scratch state in DocsHome scratch, and large corpora in DataHome.
principles.title: Put knowledge on its canonical plane

principles.text: Re-read the backend or canonical path and distinguish refresh or synchronization failure from absent content.
principles.title: Read back authoritative storage

principles.text: Route normal product model calls through LLMService; only the named streaming, embedding, and OSS-harness carve-outs bypass it.
principles.title: Product LLM calls use LLMService

principles.text: Use current routing configuration plus explicit operator overrides; Codex uses OAuth surfaces and frontier models stay in assigned roles.
principles.title: Route by role from current configuration

principles.text: An implementer does not grade itself; validation and judging use independent contexts and current artifacts.
principles.title: Separate maker checker and evidence

principles.text: Never add a global scanner skip or agent-authored suppression; unresolved exceptions are bounded human policy decisions after real fixes.
principles.title: Fix security findings structurally

principles.text: Never use shell=True, eval, exec, unsafe YAML, untrusted pickle, hardcoded secrets, or dynamic SQL concatenation; validate untrusted inputs.
principles.title: Safe execution and parsing floor

principles.text: Prefer provisioned GCT, GitHub App, service-account, or daemon paths and stop after three failed headless login attempts in sixty seconds.
principles.title: Credentials are mediated and throttled

principles.text: Use GCT discovery and invocation for brokered services, keep credentials downstream, and require a named current carve-out for direct access.
principles.title: GCT owns its domains

principles.text: Python repository acceptance uses Pants for lint, type checking, and tests; direct tools are diagnostic supplements.
principles.title: Use the canonical build surface

principles.text: Use deterministic tools for mechanical work, a direct model for one judgment, Foreman before fan-out, and Deepwork for long recursive work.
principles.title: Match task shape to mechanism

principles.text: Acceptance follows the real entry point and checks the user-visible or persisted effect, not only a return, mock call, compile, or synthetic path.
principles.title: Validate behavior not an oracle

principles.text: Unit tests may replace expensive external boundaries with constrained doubles but never mock the subject or its internal chain.
principles.title: Test doubles stay at external boundaries

principles.text: Read package hubs, accepted ADRs, changelogs, error logs, and regression ledgers before significant edits and add regression tests for repaired failures.
principles.title: Preserve regression knowledge
